item.title: After data breach, $10B valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world. Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.

item.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

item.title: You can now edit your Instagram comments
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Instagram users can now edit comments, which is a long-awaited fix for anyone who’s ever had to delete and re-post a comment just to fix a typo.

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Last month, Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent designed to build other agents. With this “agent as a service” tool, the startup intends to replace traditional click-based web applications with natural language. Users simply describe what they need, prompting Ghostwriter to autonomously create and deploy a specialized agent to execute the task.

item.title: Avec’s Tinder-style email app allows you to swipe through your inbox
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Users can use the in-built voice transcription tool to reply to emails

item.title: X brings back Voice Notes to X Chat
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X Chat brings back Voice Notes to DMs and group chats after removing the option during the upgrade.

item.title: Hacker stole £700,000 from UK energy company by redirecting payment
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The U.K. energy company said a redirected payment meant for a contractor instead landed in a hacker's bank account.

item.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter reads something like a diss track to a wide range of competitors as he defends spending $200 billion in capex.

item.title: Final 2 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Ticket discounts of up to $500 will end tomorrow, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 go up again. Miss this, and you’ll be paying more for the same access to one of the most anticipated tech events of the year. Register now to lock in these savings.

item.title: Spotify now lets everyone turn off videos in its app
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The controls can be used to choose an audio-first experience on the app or a video-enhanced one, Spotify says.

item.title: Instagram expands its movie-inspired content restrictions for teens internationally
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Instagram debuted its movie-rating-inspired content settings in limited countries in 2025.

item.title: This founder helped build SpaceX’s most powerful rocket engine. Now he’s building a ‘fighter jet for orbit.’
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company's novel rocket engine could be a game changer for the U.S. military.

item.title: Collide Capital raises $95M fund to back fintech, future-of-work startups
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Collide Capital, founded by Brian Hollins and Aaron Samuels, announced the close of a $95 million Fund II.

item.title: Radify’s sci-fi plasma reactors could break China’s dominance of rare-earth elements
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Radify Metals is developing a new way to process a variety of metals that promises to be pollution free.

item.title: Waymo robotaxis are tracking potholes and sharing that data with Waze users
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Waymo and Waze announced a data-sharing pilot program that will funnel pothole data collected by robotaxis to a free Waze platform designed for cities.

item.title: Ex-Tesla engineer’s startup taps Pronto to help automate a copper mine
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Pronto's autonomous haulage trucks are about to start operating at Mariana Minerals' Utah copper mine -- the first such deal since Pronto got acquired by Travis Kalanick's Atoms Inc.

item.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The popular open source VPN maker is the second high-profile developer to say Microsoft locked his account without notifying him and is blocking their ability to send software updates to users.
